name: refactor-safely
summary: Restructure code without changing behavior — baseline, characterize, refactor in small steps, verify.
when_to_use: Any refactor where rollback cost is high. NOT for renames covered by IDE tooling.

phases.name: Baseline
steps: Identify exact scope — files, functions, types affected.
Run test suite — confirm green before starting.
Check coverage for affected code. If < 80%, write missing tests first.

phases.name: Characterize
steps: List every call site (Grep on function/class name).
List every import of changed modules.
Identify reflection, dynamic dispatch, string-based lookups that grep misses.

phases.name: Refactor in small steps
steps: One structural change at a time.
After each change: typecheck + run affected tests.
Never leave codebase broken between steps.

phases.name: Verify
steps: Re-read final version — does it actually read better?

exit_criteria: Tests green pre and post, commits are atomic, no missed call sites, code demonstrably more readable.
anti_patterns: Big-bang refactor — hard to bisect if tests break.
Grepping for wrong pattern — missing dynamic usages.
No baseline — can't tell if refactor broke something.
